title: pterophylla eymana
side_effects: Pterophylla eymana can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, dizziness, and stomach pain, which can be caused by ingesting too much of the plant's extract. In some cases, prolonged use of the plant's essential oils can lead to allergic reactions, including skin irritation, itching, and respiratory problems. Additionally, using the plant's extracts as a medicinal treatment without proper guidance can interact with other medications, resulting in adverse reactions such as headaches, fatigue, and changes in blood pressure. Furthermore, using the plant improperly can also lead to long-term health issues, including kidney damage and liver dysfunction.
